original: Godfried II the Young becomes Duke of Brabant
Friuli Earthquake in |European southern Alps measuring approximately 6.9, largely destroys Villach, killing 5,000
Alfonso II succeeds his father as King of Naples
City of São Paulo founded in Brazil
Sir Thomas Wyatt gathers an army in Kent, rebels against Queen Mary
Battle of Talikota: The Deccan Sultanates destroy Vijayanagar's army and the last Hindu kingdom of Southern India
Pieter van den Broecke arrives at Arabian port of Mocha, will be first Dutchman to establish trade links between East India Company and Yemen and taste coffee
Duchy of Savoy orders expulsion or conversion of non-Catholic householders in Waldensian communities in Piedmont

Americans drag a cannon up a hill of Kingsbridge Road in the Bronx, New York to fight the British, giving added significance to the area's historic name of Greate Gunn Hill; the name of the road was changed to Gun Hill Road in 1875
Shays' Rebellion suffers a setback when debt-ridden farmers, led by Capt Daniel Shays, fail to capture an arsenal at Springfield, Massachusetts
The London Corresponding Society is founded.
1st US patent for a seeding machine, Eliakim Spooner, Vermont
1st US engineering college opens, Rensselaer Polytechnic, Troy, NY
Vincenzo Bellini's opera "I Puritani" premieres in Paris
Recontre between Reps Weller & Shriver, US House of Representative
Battle of Seattle; skirmish between settlers & Indians
Augusta Arsenal seized by the Confederacy in Georgia (US Civil War)
General Joseph Hooker replaces Burnside as head of Army of Potomac
CSS Shenandoah arrives in Melbourne, Australia
Soda fountain patented by Gustavus Dows
Anti-slavery society forms in New York
The Bulgarian National Bank is founded
Bilu, a Russian Zionist organization, forms
United Mine Workers of America forms
New Haven Symphony Orchestra of Connecticut performs its first concert
Aleksandr Skriabin's 2nd Symphony in C premieres in St Petersburg, Russia
179 die in coal mine explosion at Cheswick, Pennsylvania
John Millington Synge's play "Riders to the Sea" premieres in Dublin
Del Valle Inclans "El Marqués de Bradomin" premieres in Madrid
Julia Ward Howe is first woman elected to National Institute of Arts & Letters (USA)
John Blockx's opera "Baldie" premieres in Antwerp
1st stumping by a 12th man in Tests (N C Tufnell, SAf v Eng)
Children initiate idea of planting trees in Jerusalem
Transcontinental telephone service officially inaugurated asAlexander Graham Bellin NYC calls Thomas Watson in San Francisco, California during  the Panama–Pacific International Exposition
Umberto Giordano and Renato Simoni's operatic adaptation of "Madame Sans Gêne", a 1893 play by Victorien Sardou and Émile Moreau, premieres at the Metropolitan Opera in NYC
Montenegro surrenders to Austria-Hungary
Russia declared a republic of Soviets
Founding of League of Nations, 1st meeting 1 year later
The Hotel Pennsylvania, at the time the world's largest, opens in Manhattan, across Seventh from Pennsylvania Station and Madison Square Garden; closed 2020, demolished 2022-23
NVV donates 100,000 guilders to mine workers of Ruhrgebied
1st commencement exercises at Hebrew U in Jerusalem
1st broadcast of "Guiding Light" on NBC radio
Miami-to-Tampa bus overturned in a canal, kills 13
Ian Hay's "Bachelor Born" premieres in NYC
Earthquake hits Chillan Chile, 10,000 killed
Nazi decrees establishment of Jewish ghetto in Lodz Poland
Grand Rapids, Michigan, becomes 1st US city to fluoridate its water
Japanese occupiers of Batavia arrest Indo-European youths
NY Yankees sold by Ruppert estate to construction magnate Del Webb and partners Dan Topping and Larry MacPhail for $2.8 million
West Africa 82nd division occupies Myohaung, Burma
Richard Strauss' composition "Metamorphosen" premieres with the Collegium Musicum in Zürich, Switzerland
United Mine Workers union rejoins American Federation of Labor
1st Emmy Awards: Shirley Dinsdale & Pantomime Quiz (KTLA) win
73°F (23°C) highest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland in January
WABI TV channel 5 in Bangor, ME (CBS) begins broadcasting
Yuri Sergejev skates world record 500m in 40.9 sec
American alpine skier Jill Kinmont hits a tree & breaks her back in Snow Cup Ski Race
Columbia University scientists develop an atomic clock accurate to within one second in 300 years
Russia ends state of war with Germany
US & Panama sign canal treaty
96.5 cm (38.0") of rainfall in a day, Kilauea Plantation, Hawaii (state record, until 2018)
FBI arrests Jack & Myra Sobel, charged with spying for USSR
1st transcontinental commercial jet flight (American) (LA to NY for $301)
Military coup in El Salvador
Echo 2, US communications satellite launched
Nike athletics company founded in Oregon as Blue Ribbon Sports by University of Oregon track athlete Phil Knight and his coach, Bill Bowerman
The Beatles get their first US #1, "I Want to Hold Your Hand" (Cashbox)
WCMC (now WMGM) TV channel 40 in Wildwood, NJ (NBC) 1st broadcast
Risse St in Bronx named
Robert Anderson's "I Never Sang for My Father" premieres in NYC
US-North Vietnamese peace talks begin in Paris
Himachal Pradesh becomes 18th Indian state
WHMB TV channel 40 in Indianapolis, IN (IND) begins broadcasting
Muriel Humphrey (D-Mn) appointed to fill late husband's Senate seat
22.2-km Oshimizu railroad tunnel holed through, central Honshu, Japan
First documented case of a robot killing a human in the US
Bani Sadr elected president of Iran
Dutch government demands boycott of Olympics
Highest speed attained by a warship, 167 kph, USN hovercraft
Super Bowl XV, Louisiana Superdome, New Orleans, LA: Oakland Raiders beat Philadelphia Eagles, 27-10; MVP: Jim Plunkett, Oakland, QB
China's supreme court commutes Chiang Ch'ing's death sentence to life
Infrared telescope satellite launched into polar orbit
"Black & Blue" premieres in Paris
General Tito Okello's government flees Kampala, Uganda
Longest winless streak in Toronto Maple Leaf history (15 games)
Ramsewak Shankar sworn in as President of Suriname
Augusto Alcalde, 1st South American Zen teacher, receives Dharma Transmission
Avianca Flight 052 runs out of fuel and crashes into a hillside at Cove Neck, New York, killing 8 of the 9 crew members and 65 of the 149 passengers on board
Honduras becomes a member of the Berne Convention copyright treaty
The Burns' Day windstorm storm hits northwestern Europe
Manuel Noriegais given access to assets frozen by US government
Soap opera "Generation's" last episode after a 2½ year run
Hubble space telescope optics finds NGC3862/3C264
Five people are shot outside CIA headquarters in Langley, Virginia, resulting in two deaths
Puerto Rico adds English as its 2nd official language
Sears announces it is closing its catalog sales department after 97 years

US space probe Clementine launched from Vandenberg Air Force Base, California
Chicago Blackhawks beat Edmonton Oilers, 5-1 in the first NHL game in their new home, the United Center; defenseman Chris Chelios leads scoring with a goal and 2 assists
Jacques Santer of Luxembourg succeeds Jacques Delors of France as President of the European Commission
Ottawa Senators set an NHL record with 16th consecutive home game without a win (0-15-1) in a 4-2 loss to Detroit at the Palladium; old record (15) shared by 1928 Black Hawks & 1939 Canadiens

In a high profile NHL trade, San Jose obtain future Hockey Hall of Fame goaltender Ed Belfour from Chicago in exchange for goaltender Chris Terreri, Ulf Dahlen, Michal Sykora and a conditional pick in 1998 Entry Draft
"Patti LaBelle On Broadway" closes at St James Theater NYC
Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam mount a suicide attack on Sri Lanka's Temple of the Tooth in Kandy, killing 8 people and injuring 25 others
PopeJohn Paul IIvisits Cuba and demands the release of political prisoners; he also condemns US moves to isolate the country
Super Bowl XXXII, Qualcomm Stadium, San Diego, CA: Denver Broncos beat Green Bay Packers, 31-24; MVP: Terrell Davis, Denver RB
After an inquiry into a corruption scandal, 6 International Olympic Committee members are expelled
Earthquake hits Colombia, South America, killing around 300 and injuring 1000
A 50-year-old Douglas DC-3 crashes near Ciudad Bolivar, Venezuela killing 24
Wikipedia switches to a new version of its software ("Phase II"), also known as Magnus Manske Day
A stampede at the Mandher Devi temple in Mandhradevi in India kills at least 258
Three independent observing campaigns announce the discovery of OGLE-2005-BLG-390Lb through gravitational microlensing, the first cool rocky/icy extrasolar planet around a main-sequence star.
Ethiopian Airlines Flight 409 crashes into the Mediterranean Sea shortly after take-off from Beirut Rafic Hariri International Airport, killing all 90 people on-board.
Egyptian Revolution of 2011 begins with a series of street demonstrations, rallies, acts of civil disobedience, labor strikes and violent clashes in Cairo, Alexandria and other major cities
UK announces it had a shock economic contraction of 0.5% in Q4 2010, due to severe winter weather and budget cuts implemented by the coalition government
50 people are killed and 90 are injured in a prison riot in Barquisimeto, Venezuela
6 civilians and 1 police officer are shot dead and 456 people are injured during a nationwide protest against Egypt’s 2011 revolution
8 people are killed by 2 car bombs in Golan Heights, Syria
Initial GDP figures for the fourth quarter of 2012 show the UK economy shrank by 0.3% raising fears of a triple dip recession
Islamist forces are driven out of Hombori by the Malian army
Tropical cyclone Oswald makes landfall in Queensland, Australia, causing mass flooding
British rowing team becomes first female crew and first crew of four to cross the Pacific, San Francisco to Cairns in 257 days
Kisenosato becomes the 1st Japanese home-grown sumo champion since 1998 when he is made yokozuna, 72nd Grand Champion
Doomsday clock moved by 30 seconds to 2 minutes to midnight by Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists, closest since 1950s
Nigerian author Chimamanda Ngozi asked "are there any bookshops in Nigeria?" in controversial French interview
At least 20 people have died this week in Venezuelan anti-government protests according to the UN
Roger Stone, political lobbyist forDonald Trump, is arrested as part of Mueller investigation for obstruction of justice, witness tampering and making false statements
Tailing dam for an iron ore mine in Brumadinho, south-eastern Brazil, collapses killing at least 110 with 238 missing
Severe rainstorms cause landslides and floods in Brazil's Minas Gerais state, killing at least 30
Australian government buys the copyright to the Aboriginal flag designed by artist Harold Thomas for A$20 million (US$14m)
People smuggling boat reported to have capsized in the Florida Straits with the loss of 38 lives

Philadelphia Flyers defenseman Keith Yandle becomes NHL's new "Iron Man" with his 965th consecutive regular-season game, breaking Doug Jarvis's all-time record, in a 4-3 loss at the New York Islanders
America and Germany announce decision to send powerful battle tanks (US 31, Germany 14) to Ukraine to fight Russia invasion

British researchers Christopher Marsh and Angela McShane launch website 100Ballads.org detailing top-selling broadside ballads of 17th century England, including images of original lyric sheets, and new musical recordings of the very old pop songs
After a night of negotiation,Claudiusis accepted as Roman Emperor by the Senate
KingEdward IIIaccedes to the English throne aged 14, after his mother, Isabella of France, and her lover Roger Mortimer depose his father Edward II
Battle of Mikatagahara, in Japan; Takeda Shingen defeatsTokugawa Ieyasu
French theologianJacques-Bénigne Bossuetobtains his first thesis in theology with Louis II de Bourbon, Prince of Condé, as a witness
TsarPeter the Greatmakes the Moscow patriarchate and Russian Orthodox Church subordinate to the state
Moscow University is established byElizabeth of Russiaand founded by Mikhail Lomonosov on Tatiana Day
Napoleon Bonaparteelected president of Italian (Cisalpine) Republic
Gioachino Rossini's opera "La Cenerentola" (Cinderella) premieres at Rome's Teatro Valle
University of Virginia chartered by Commonwealth of Virginia, withThomas Jeffersonone of its founders
William Henry Fox Talbotexhibits early photographs to the Royal Institution in the UK
American naval expedition underCharles Wilkesis first to identify Antarctica as a new continent
Aleksandr Ostrovsky's play "Bednost ne Porok" premieres in Moscow
Felix Mendelssohn's "Wedding March" is first played at the wedding ofQueen Victoria's daughter, Princess Victoria, and the Crown Prince of Prussia
Congress establishes the Electoral Commission to determine the disputed presidential election betweenRutherford B. Hayesand Samuel J. Tilden
Thomas EdisonandAlexander Graham Bellform the Oriental Telephone Company.
Vincent d'Indy's symphonic poem "Saugefleurie" premieres with the Concerts Lamoureux in Paris
JournalistNellie Blybeats the fictional journey ofJules Verne's Phileas Fogg around the world by 8 days, completing it in 72 days
National Afro-American League founded in Chicago byTimothy Thomas Fortune, one of earliest civil rights organizations in America
In his only successful title defense,James J CorbettKOs English champion Charley Mitchell in 3 rounds at the Duvall Athletic Club, Jacksonville, Florida for the world heavyweight boxing title
Arnold Schoenberg's symphonic poem "Pelleas und Melissande" premieres at the Musikverein in Vienna, Austria
Richard Strauss' one-act opera "Elektra" premieres at the Königliches Opernhaus in Dresden, Germany

An Indian mass meeting in Durban, South Africa unanimously endorses the agreement between GeneralJan SmutsandMahatma Gandhiregarding voluntary registration, poll tax, recognition of Indian marriages and other matters
Karel Čapek's play "R.U.R." premieres in Prague, introduces the word "robot"
1st nuclear fission experiment (splitting of a uranium atom) in the US, in basement of Pupin Hall, Columbia University by a team includingEnrico Fermi
In only the second World Heavyweight C'ship fight between two black boxers,Joe LouisKOs John Henry Lewis in 1 at Madison Square Garden, NYC
PopePius XIIelevates the Apostolic Vicariate of the Hawaiian Islands to the dignity of a diocese. It becomes the Roman Catholic Diocese of Honolulu.

First Israeli election won byDavid Ben-Gurion's Mapai party
Test debut of great Australian cricket captain (62 Tests) and broadcasterRichie Benaud, v West Indies at the SCG
PopeJohn XXIIIproclaims 2nd Vatican council
Wilt Chamberlainscores 58 points, the most ever by an NBA rookie, as Philadelphia Warriors beat Detroit Pistons, 127-117 at Bethlehem, PA
First live, nationally televised presidential news conference, held byJFK
Walt Disney's animated film "101 Dalmatians", based on the novel byDodie Smithand directed by Clyde Geronimi and Hamilton Luske is released in the US
Dark comedy film "M*A*S*H", directed byRobert Altman, starringDonald SutherlandandElliott Gould, and based on novel by Richard Hooker, premieres (Palme d'Or 1970)
Charles Mansonand three female followers are convicted of the Tate-LaBianca murders
Military coup in Uganda led by Major GeneralIdi Amin
Philadelphia Mint's first trial strike of theEisenhowerdollar

Michael Jordanscores his 10,000th NBA point in his 5th season

Former Panamanian leaderManuel Noriegais transferred to a Miami jail
